title: hymenophyllum subobtusum
intro: Hymenophyllum subobtusum, also known as the Hairy Forked Rock Fern, is a species of fern native to Australia. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hymenophyllum subobtusum include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The plant can be prepared into a herbal tea or infused oil, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments in Australian indigenous medicine.
